items.title: Stacks & Queues
items.description: A priority queue replaces 'oldest first' (FIFO) with 'most important first' — same ADT family, different ordering policy.

items.title: Trees & BSTs
items.description: Both are binary trees; heaps enforce parent ≤ children (no key order between siblings), BSTs enforce left < node < right.

items.title: Sorting Algorithms
items.description: Heap-sort uses heapify + repeated heappop to produce a sorted sequence in O(n log n) — a classic heap application.

items.title: Graphs
items.description: Dijkstra and A* both use a priority queue to expand the most promising frontier node next.

items.title: Big-O Complexity
items.description: The classic O(log n) sift and the O(n) heapify illustrate amortized vs per-op analysis nicely.
